body:
FINAL INSTRUCTION: Complete 9.40 thoroughly as per all prior instructions. This is the definitive build.

CONTEXT RECAP (everything agreed and instructed):

1. METHODOLOGY FRAMEWORK (apply to every subsystem):
   WHAT does this logic actually do step by step — not what the comment says, what the code does.
   WHY is it doing it this way — what assumption about markets does this rest on.
   IS THAT ASSUMPTION CORRECT — is this the right procedure for what this phase is trying to accomplish.
   WHAT INFORMATION is this phase trying to extract — is the extraction method giving the richest most regime-aware most actionable intelligence or a one-size-fits-all output that loses signal in aggregation.
   WHAT WOULD A BETTER VERSION LOOK LIKE — practical, implementable, coherent with the whole system.

2. PHASES MUST LEARN FROM DIFFERENT LOCATIONS IN THE MARKET:
   Each calibration phase (1 through 6) should sample from distinct, complementary locations in price/time/regime space. Not the same series at different resolutions — genuinely different aspects of market structure. If a phase is currently extracting the same class of information as another phase, that is a redundancy to fix. Each phase should produce intelligence the others cannot.

3. CORRELATIONS AND RELATIONSHIPS:
   The system should model meaningful relationships between components, between timeframes, between current structure and historical analogues. The correlation architecture must be specific to what TUNGSTEN does — identify exploitable edge in a live FX instrument — not generic ML theory applied blindly.

4. GROWTH RATE:
   Replace hardcoded 0.42 with dynamic derivation from live regime correlation data — actual rolling win rate and RR per regime, actual half-Kelly — as already proposed and approved. Show the derivation in the relay report.

5. DetectCurrentRegime() correlation-model override:
   Resolve the intentional-vs-unfinished question. Read the surrounding logic carefully. If it is a confirmation-only signal that was designed to not write back, document why in a comment and leave it. If it is unfinished wiring, finish it. Do not leave it ambiguous.

6. THE STANDARD:
   9.40 must be exponentially better than 9.37 as a system. Not larger, not more complex — sharper intelligence, tighter calibration, fewer blindspots, sounder methodology. Every improvement must make the system better as a whole. A fix that makes one subsystem locally correct but breaks coherence with adjacent logic is not an improvement.

7. THE THESIS:
   Write the full 5-section thesis via relay BEFORE implementing — as instructed in message dca841dc. The thesis is the blueprint. Every design decision in 9.40 must trace back to a specific argument in the thesis. Send thesis first, then implement.

DELIVERABLES:
- Thesis sent via relay (multiple messages if needed) before any code is written
- 9.40 compiled clean (0 errors)
- 9.40 .mq4 in files/from-agent/ with manifest
- Full written assessment via relay: what changed, what assumptions were wrong, what blindspots were found, what the system looks like now versus 9.37

Work autonomously to completion. This is the most important build pass TUNGSTEN has had. Do it thoroughly.
